differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., renal cell carcinoma, lung adenocarcinoma)
next_steps: Perform cardiac MRI to assess tissue characteristics and extent of infiltration
Perform PET-CT scan to evaluate metabolic activity and search for a primary malignancy
Perform image-guided biopsy of the most accessible peripheral or nodal lesion (mediastinal lymph node or lung nodule) rather than the cardiac mass
Continue systemic anticoagulation with careful monitoring due to the risk of pulmonary embolism and potential for tumor-associated bleeding
Consult cardiothoracic oncology to discuss the risks and benefits of surgical tissue sampling if non-invasive biopsy remains inconclusive
